image_prompt: SVG: Two animal-cell models side by side. LEFT — 3D physical-model style photo on black background, translucent blue cytoplasm, pink nucleus, pink mitochondria, blue ribosomes, beige ER. RIGHT — 2D textbook illustration, yellow cytoplasm, purple nucleus with nucleolus, blue mitochondria with cristae, red lysosomes.

SVG: Draggable-label diagram. Cell with nucleus on left zooms into an X-shaped chromosome in the middle, which zooms into a double-helix DNA molecule on the right; empty label boxes around each (Chromosome, DNA, Gene, Nucleus, Nucleotide) and pink draggable-label chips at top.
SVG: Portrait of a smiling man in a teal polo, head-and-shoulders, with a visible cheek dimple; label 'Dimple' on the left with an arrow pointing to the dimple.
SVG: Empty 3x3 Punnett-square table. Top header 'Father's alleles' with column labels D and d; left header 'Mother's alleles'. Body cells blank for student to complete.

SVG: Forest food web. Producers (Grass, Oak tree, Acorns) at bottom; primary consumers (Deer, Rabbit, Squirrel) middle; secondary consumers (Fox, Owl, Snake) upper; apex predator (Black bear) at top. Arrows go from prey to predator following: Grass->Deer; Grass->Rabbit; Oak tree/Acorns->Squirrel; Rabbit->Fox, Owl; Squirrel->Owl, Snake; Deer->Bear; Fox->Bear; Owl->Bear.

SVG: bar chart 'How different aquatic plants affect the survival of prey'. Y-axis 'Percentage average survival / %' 0-80 (ticks every 10). Four green bars: Plant A (No plants) = 30, Plant B (Native plants) = 55, Plant C = 71, Plant D (Non-native plants) = 41; grouping labels No plants / Native plants / Non-native plants.
SVG: Photosynthesis equation drag-and-drop. Four empty slots arranged as [ ] + [ ] -> [ ] + [ ], with a sun icon above the arrow. Draggable green chips at top: Carbon dioxide, Glucose, Oxygen, Water.

topic: Lab notebook — method for Cabomba photosynthesis
image_prompt: SVG: Spiral-bound lab notebook page, ruled paper with red margin and spiral binding holes on the left. Title 'Method' (italic). Six numbered steps in handwriting-style font describing the Cabomba/funnel/measuring-cylinder photosynthesis investigation, with a blank line for Step 4's missing equipment.

SVG: Data table 'Number of gas bubbles produced in 5 minutes'. Columns: Temperature, Trial 1, Trial 2, Trial 3, Trial 4, Average. Rows: 20 / 5 / 7 / 5 / 7 / (blank); 40 / 6 / 13 / 14 / 12 / 11.25; 50 / 2 / 1 / 2 / 1 / 1.5.

topic: Effect of temperature on photosynthesis rate
image_prompt: SVG: line graph 'Effect of temperature on average number of gas bubbles produced in 5 minutes'. Y-axis 'Average number of bubbles in 5 minutes' 0-12 (ticks every 2). X-axis 'Temperature / °C' 0-60 (ticks every 10; x labels reconstructed from data table as scan truncated them). Curve rises from (20,6) to a peak ~(32,11.5) then falls to (50,2) — denaturation above optimum.

SVG: Enzyme lock-and-key diagram. Small key-shaped substrate + large pacman-style enzyme with a wedge cutout -> enzyme-substrate complex (key seated in wedge) + small diamond product. Empty dropdown boxes under each shape for labelling (Enzyme / Substrate / Product / Carbohydrate).

SVG: Two-panel infographic. LEFT 'during the day' on white with a sun behind a cloud, dashed orange curves for cortisol (stressed = long dashes peaking high, non-stressed = short dashes peaking lower), solid blue melatonin low; lightning bolt + 'High cortisol ->' label + 'active'. RIGHT 'during the night' on dark blue sky with stars and full moon, melatonin curve peaking high; 'Zzz'; 'High melatonin -> sleep' label. Key on far left.

SVG: Two-column flowchart with rounded coloured boxes. Top teal box 'Scientists induce stress in two groups of lab rats' branches into two columns. Left column (Group A, melatonin): Their cortisol levels rise -> One group is given melatonin -> Monitor the sleep pattern over several weeks (pink) -> Monitor the health of the rats and any side effects (green). Right column (Group B, control): Their cortisol levels rise -> Monitor the sleep pattern over several weeks (pink) -> Monitor the health of the rats (green). Connected with arrows.
